name: Clash Royale
description: Clash Royale is a real-time strategy game where two players deploy units, spells, and buildings from an 8-card deck onto a two-lane battlefield to destroy opponent towers within a 3-minute match, plus a 1-minute overtime. Players spend elixir, which regenerates at a steady rate (e.g., 1 elixir every 2.8 seconds), to deploy cards, each with unique elixir costs (1-10), health, and damage values. Progression is driven by a trophy-based ranking system, where winning battles earns trophies and reaching specific trophy thresholds unlocks new Arenas, introducing new sets of collectible cards. Cards can be upgraded using collected duplicates and gold currency, increasing their combat statistics. The user interface involves tapping and dragging cards from a hand at the bottom of the screen to deploy them onto the player's side of the arena. Victory is achieved by destroying the opponent's King Tower or having more towers standing when the timer expires.
reasoning: Clash Royale, despite being a real-time strategy game, is a good candidate. Its core mechanics (elixir generation, card deployment, unit movement on a two-lane battlefield, combat logic, and tower destruction) are all implementable with standard web technologies. Graphics can easily be simplified from 3D models to 2D sprites or simple shapes without losing the essence of the game. While the original is multiplayer, the opponent can be replaced by a reasonably 'simple rule-based AI bot' that manages elixir, deploys cards, and targets units/towers. Physics (Matter.js) could be used for unit collision or projectile trajectories, but isn't strictly required for complex rigid-body dynamics, making it acceptable. Progression systems (trophies, arenas, card upgrades) can be simplified significantly for a generator project. It avoids the pitfalls of highly complex simulations, MMOs, or games requiring photo-realistic 3D graphics.
